{"id":"project-LICHEN-a50r","title":"TUI refresh_rf_health has unsafe type conversion from untrusted dict","description":"file: python/src/lichen/tui/native.py:2251-2258

Similar to the issue in refresh_radio (cs4q), refresh_rf_health extracts values from an untrusted dict without type validation:

  rf_info = radio_info.get('rf_health', radio_info.get('local_rf', {}))
  local_rf = LocalRFStats(
      noise_floor_dbm=rf_info.get('noise_floor_dbm'),  # Could be any type
      channel_busy_pct=rf_info.get('channel_busy_pct'),  # Could be any type
      rx_crc_errors=int(rf_info.get('rx_crc_errors', 0)),  # Crashes on non-int value
      rx_timeout_errors=int(rf_info.get('rx_timeout_errors', 0)),  # Crashes on non-int value
      rx_header_errors=int(rf_info.get('rx_header_errors', 0)),  # Crashes on non-int value
      rx_total=int(rf_info.get('rx_total', 0)),  # Crashes on non-int value
  )

If the server returns unexpected types (e.g., rx_crc_errors='unknown'), int() raises ValueError and crashes the RF Health screen refresh.

Should wrap conversions in try/except and use default values on failure.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:07:03.048804Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T17:07:03.048837Z"}
